indentation-and-blocks.label: Indentation and Blocks
indentation-and-blocks.summary: Understand why indentation controls structure in Python.

q1.title: Indentation in Control Flow
q1.prompt: Write a program that checks if a number is positive, negative, or zero, and prints the appropriate message.
q1.hint: Focus on the programming task being asked for, not on copying final solution text.

help.concept: Build the solution from the behavior the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which steps, functions, or statements are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the solution based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q1.starterCode:
number = float(input('Enter a number: '))

# Your code here

q2.title: Nested Conditions
q2.prompt: Create a program that checks if a number is even or odd and also checks if it is positive or negative.
q2.hint: Focus on the programming task being asked for, not on copying final solution text.

help.concept: Build the solution from the behavior the exercise is testing.
help.hint_1: Think about which steps, functions, or statements are required for the task.
help.hint_2: Construct the solution based on what result the exercise expects, not by repeating exact solution wording.

q2.starterCode:
number = int(input('Enter an integer: '))

# Your code here

q3.title: Purpose of Indentation
q3.prompt: What is the primary purpose of indentation in Python?
q3.hint: Think about how Python interprets code structure.

help.concept: Indentation in Python is crucial for defining the structure of code blocks, especially in control flow statements.
help.hint_1: It helps Python understand which statements belong to which block.
help.hint_2: Without proper indentation, Python will raise an IndentationError.

q4.title: Identifying Indentation Errors
q4.prompt: Which of the following will cause an IndentationError in Python? Select all that apply.

help.concept: An IndentationError occurs when the indentation levels are inconsistent within a block of code.
help.hint_1: Check for lines that are not aligned properly.
help.hint_2: Remember that all lines in a block must have the same indentation level.

q5.title: Fill in the Blank: Indentation
q5.prompt: In Python, indentation is used to define __________.
q5.hint: Think about how blocks of code are structured.

help.concept: Indentation defines the scope of loops, conditionals, and functions in Python.
help.hint_1: It helps Python know which statements belong together.
help.hint_2: Without it, Python cannot determine the structure of your code.

q5.template: In Python, indentation is used to define {blank}.

1.title: Understanding Indentation
1.bodyMarkdown: In Python, indentation is not just for readability; it defines the structure of the code. Blocks of code are determined by their indentation level.

2.title: Blocks and Control Flow
2.bodyMarkdown: Control flow statements like if, elif, and else rely on indentation to determine which statements belong to which block.
